reasoning: Dan Osborn officially formed an exploratory committee for the 2026 Senate race on April 3, 2025. According to reports, he indicated that he "could make a decision on his candidacy by the end of this month [April] or early next month [May]." Exploratory committees for high-profile candidates—especially those coming off strong recent campaigns like Osborn's near-miss in 2024, where he raised $14 million and lost by roughly six points—almost always lead to official campaign announcements. Given his explicit timeframe of late-April to early-May and the resolution deadline of June 1, he has over a full month beyond his "early next month" target to make a formal announcement. The main risks are normal campaign delays (e.g., waiting to launch at a more strategic time in the summer) or an unexpected decision not to run, but his pivot away from a crowded House race towards the Senate makes a run very likely. Therefore, I estimate a 77% chance he meets the June 1 deadline.
batch_queries: Will Dan Osborn announce his candidacy for US Senator from Nebraska before June 1, 2025?
